forecast-period.text: A high pressure system southwest of the Bight extends a ridge the west. The high will dominate the weather pattern over the weekend as it slowly moves east, reaching the Tasman Sea by Tuesday.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Isolated light showers about the southern agricultural area, most likely about windward coasts and ranges. Morning fog patches about the agricultural area and south of the pastoral districts. Maximum temperatures below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds.

Isolated light showers about southern coastal parts in the morning. Mostly dry during the day. Morning frost and fog patches about the agricultural area. Maximum temperatures below average. Southeast to northeasterly winds.
Mostly dry. Possible morning frost and fog on and east of the ranges. Maximum temperatures below average, grading to above average in the southeast. Southeast to northeasterly winds.

text.p: Chance of showers about central pastoral districts. Maximum temperatures below average grading to above average in the east. Southeast to northeasterly winds.
Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Tuesday: Less than 2 mm generally about the agricultural area and central parts of the pastoral districts, with isolated falls of up to 5mm.
